body:
## Summary
- render accessibility-store persistence warnings on Settings with the accessibility-preferences copy scope and dismiss handler
- keep Settings on the shared theme context while preserving local theme controls
- preserve recoverable progress read warnings for corrupt persisted JSON and update validator mirrors

## Verification
- `NODE_PATH=/home/billy/Swedish_Civic_Test/node_modules PATH=/home/billy/Swedish_Civic_Test/node_modules/.bin:$PATH NODE_OPTIONS='--v8-pool-size=1' npm run validate:content`
- `NODE_PATH=/home/billy/Swedish_Civic_Test/node_modules PATH=/home/billy/Swedish_Civic_Test/node_modules/.bin:$PATH NODE_OPTIONS='--v8-pool-size=1' node --test tests/content-settings-route-copy-parity.test.js tests/content-storage-write-fail-soft.test.js`
- `ln -s /home/billy/Swedish_Civic_Test/node_modules node_modules && NODE_OPTIONS='--v8-pool-size=1' npm run typecheck -- --pretty false && rm node_modules`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`

Note: `tests/v1-1-accessibility.test.js` still has a pre-existing failure in the separate app-wide dark-theme migration guard for `app/(tabs)/practice.tsx` using static colors; this branch updates Settings to use the shared theme context but does not absorb that queued atom.
title: Surface accessibility persistence warnings in Settings
